name: L. paracasei JS-3 extended C. elegans lifespan and shifted elderly-donor microbiota in vitro
summary: JS-3 provides a current direct aging-model signal, but the elderly-human component is in vitro fermentation rather than a human intervention.
statement: Lactobacillus paracasei JS-3 increased C. elegans lifespan, stress survival, motility, and antioxidant readouts, and in vitro fermentation experiments indicated shifts in elderly-donor gut microbiota toward genera described as beneficial.
population_or_model: C. elegans aging assays and in vitro fermentation using gut microbiota from elderly individuals
time_horizon: C. elegans lifespan assay and in vitro fermentation windows reported by the source
quantitative_note: The abstract reports a 28.02% increase in mean C. elegans lifespan with JS-3, compared with a 15.34% increase for NMN in the study design.
caveats: No human ingestion trial was reported.
C. elegans lifespan and in vitro elderly-microbiota fermentation are not human aging-function evidence.
The finding supports preclinical prioritization, not a clinical stage upgrade by itself.
